proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90) is positively associated with pfs_months across the cohort.
feature_001 (ordinal 0/1/2) is negatively (monotonically) associated with pfs_months.
feature_014 (binary, ~55% prevalence) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_027 (binary, ~20% prevalence) is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.766; deciles of feature_015 show monotone increase from mean PFS 1.33 (Q1, f015 30-52) to 7.45 (Q10, f015 78-90).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Multiple binary features (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_011) show small but significant main-effect associations with pfs_months.
Continuous features feature_019, feature_025, feature_005, feature_021 are linearly correlated with pfs_months.
feature_005 with its huge range (0.02-777) is better modeled on the log scale.

analyses.code: ttest for each binary against pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=1: -0.305 (p=1e-49); feature_023=1: -0.327 (p=2e-58); feature_022=1: -0.228 (p=3e-7); feature_011=1: +0.221 (p=7e-4); rare and other binaries showed no effect.
log(feature_005) vs PFS: r=-0.063 (p=2e-45), markedly stronger than raw scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS adjusting for all features, feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_027, feature_005 (log), feature_019, feature_021 (log), and feature_025 are independently associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R²=0.861. Significant independent predictors (coef): feature_015 +0.176, feature_001 -1.17, feature_014 -1.36, feature_006 -0.29, feature_023 -0.35, feature_022 -0.35, log(feature_005) -0.116, feature_019 +0.47, log(feature_021) -0.083, feature_025 -0.076, feature_027 +0.94. All other features had p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023, feature_011, and feature_022 are mutually exclusive (no patient has more than one =1).
These three features represent a categorical biomarker classification (likely RAS/BRAF-like mutations).

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed mutually exclusive: WT (000): 25324; f023 only: 20898 (42%); f011 only: 1506 (3%); f022 only: 2272 (4.5%); zero overlap.
Mean PFS by biomarker: WT 4.48; f011 4.53; f022 4.09; f023 4.12. The three positives have similar (lower) PFS than WT.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027's treatment effect is heterogeneous across feature_023 (treatment effect smaller or absent in feature_023=1 patients).
feature_027's treatment effect is heterogeneous across feature_006 (effect smaller or absent in feature_006=1 patients).
feature_027's treatment effect is heterogeneous across feature_022 (effect smaller in feature_022=1 patients).

analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect in feature_023=0: +1.66 mo (p~6e-293); in feature_023=1: +0.03 mo (p=0.46). Interaction coef -1.63, p=8e-226.
Treatment effect in feature_006=0: +1.48 mo; feature_006=1: +0.03 mo. Interaction coef -1.45, p=4e-168.
Treatment effect in feature_022=0: +1.03; in feature_022=1: -0.16. Interaction coef -0.98, p=1e-104.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full favorable subgroup defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 captures the entire feature_027 treatment benefit; outside this subgroup, the treatment effect is null.

analyses.code: stratified mean diff by (f006, f023, f022) octants
analyses.result_summary: Eight subgroup test: f006=0,f023=0,f022=0 (n=17372): treatment effect +2.76 mo, p~0; ALL seven other subgroups have effect within [-0.20, +0.08] and p>0.10. The favorable triple condition is a clean dichotomization.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup (f006=f023=f022=0), feature_005 further modifies feature_027's benefit: treatment effect is concentrated where feature_005 is low.

analyses.code: OLS within favorable: pfs ~ feature_027 * log(feature_005)
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable, interaction feature_027:log(f005) = -1.66 (p<<1e-300). Quintile analysis shows treatment effect of +5.0/+5.1 in lowest two quintiles (f005<3.3), +3.5 in middle, ~0 in upper two (f005>=6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a sharp threshold near feature_005 = 5: below feature_005 < 5, treatment effect is ~+5 months; at or above 5, it is ~0.

analyses.code: grid search of cutoffs from 4.0 to 5.2 within favorable
analyses.result_summary: Step-function: cutoff 4.0 → below +5.04 / above +0.69 (still residual); cutoff 4.8 → +5.02 / +0.09; cutoff 5.0 → +5.01 / +0.005 (clean break); cutoff 5.2 → +4.94 / -0.008. Threshold is sharply at feature_005 = 5.0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Define eligible = (feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5). In eligible patients, feature_027 increases pfs_months by ~5 months; in ineligible patients, the treatment has no effect.

analyses.result_summary: Eligible (n=9326, 18.7% of cohort): treated mean PFS 9.10 (n=1903), untreated 4.09 (n=7423), diff +5.013, p~0. Ineligible (n=40674): diff +0.027, p=0.43.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four conditions in the eligibility definition is necessary: relaxing any one of them dilutes the treatment effect.

analyses.code: drop each predicate from definition and recompute treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Drop f006=0 → +3.30 (subgroup n=14472); drop f023=0 → +2.85 (n=16582); drop f022=0 → +4.62 (n=10100); drop f005<5 → +2.76 (n=17372). All four predicates contribute; the rare f022 contributes least but is non-redundant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, the treatment effect is independent of all other features (no further effect modifiers).

analyses.code: stratified treatment effect by quintiles of every continuous feature and by every binary, within eligible subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect within eligible is uniformly between +4.7 and +5.3 across feature_015 quintiles, feature_001 levels, feature_019/021/025/032/017/003/030/004/008/013/026/029/007/033/009 quintiles, and across all 11 other binary features. No additional effect modification detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The other binary features (feature_010, feature_024, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_028, feature_016, feature_002, feature_031) are not treatments with hidden subgroup effects.

analyses.code: for each binary, run interaction screen and search best pairwise restriction
analyses.result_summary: Best pair-restricted treatment effect for each candidate is small: feature_010 -0.03, feature_024 -0.09, feature_012 +0.08, feature_018 +0.09, feature_020 -0.11, feature_027 +2.52 (the only real treatment). No other binary shows meaningful heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (the strongest negative main effect after stage) is purely prognostic — it does not modify the effect of feature_027.

analyses.result_summary: Within eligible: f014=0 effect +5.03, f014=1 effect +5.01 (no difference). feature_014's strongest interaction across the cohort was with feature_015 (coef -0.007, p=2e-9, negligible magnitude). feature_014 is prognostic only.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (ordinal 0/1/2) is purely prognostic with a roughly linear slope of ~-1.17 mo per level after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.code: multivariable OLS with feature_001 and treatment-effect interaction
analyses.result_summary: feature_001 fully-adjusted coef = -1.166, t=-918. Strongest interaction across the cohort is with feature_015 (coef -0.007, p=1e-18; magnitude trivial). feature_001 is a strong prognostic, not a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015, when treated as a continuous prognostic, has a near-linear slope of ~+0.176 mo per unit on PFS, and explains about 59% of the variance on its own.

analyses.code: Linear, quadratic, cubic regressions of pfs_months on feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Linear R²=0.587, slope=+0.176; quadratic R²=0.588 (negligible curvature); cubic R²=0.589. Linear is appropriate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (range 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with PFS independent of all other features.
feature_025 (range 0-25, count-like) is negatively associated with PFS independent of all other features.

analyses.code: fully adjusted OLS coef of feature_019
analyses.result_summary: feature_019 fully-adjusted coef = +0.485 (t=+282, p~0).

analyses.code: fully adjusted OLS coef of feature_025
analyses.result_summary: feature_025 fully-adjusted coef = -0.078 per unit (t=-348, p~0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: log(feature_021) is mildly negatively associated with PFS, independent of other features.
Most other continuous features (003, 004, 008, 013, 017, 026, 029, 030, 032, 033, 007, 009) have no detectable independent association with PFS.

analyses.code: fully adjusted OLS coef of log(feature_021)
analyses.result_summary: log(feature_021) fully-adjusted coef = -0.097 (t=-39.5, p~0).

After full adjustment with the eligible×feature_027 interaction in the model (R²=0.993), only feature_029 (p=0.026) and feature_007 (p=0.037) reached p<0.05 with trivial coefficients (~+0.0005); all others non-significant. They are noise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full data-generating process for pfs_months is well-approximated by a linear additive model in feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, log(feature_021), and a single interaction term (eligible × feature_027).

analyses.result_summary: Model R²=0.993. Adjusted treatment effect within eligible: +4.979 [4.971, 4.987], p~0. Adjusted treatment effect within ineligible: -0.003 [-0.008, +0.002], p=0.24 (null). Constant ~ -6.74. All identified prognostic features remained significant (feature_006/023/022/log(005) became non-significant once the eligible interaction was in the model — they only mattered through the treatment interaction).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment is randomly allocated with respect to the eligibility criteria: the marginal treatment rate is ~20% inside and outside the eligible subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment rate within eligible: 20.4% (1903/9326); within ineligible: 20.0% (8119/40674). Treatment is balanced across eligibility — assignment appears randomized.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (rare 3% binary, mutually exclusive with f023/f022) confers a small unconditional positive PFS shift independent of treatment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 univariate effect +0.221 (p=7e-4); fully adjusted, the coefficient drops to ~+0.02 (p=0.37). feature_011's apparent main effect was confounded with the favorable subgroup (it overlaps with f023=0, f022=0 by mutual exclusivity).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the eligible subgroup, feature_011 does not further modify the treatment effect (treatment benefit is the same with or without f011).

analyses.code: stratified treatment effect within eligible by feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Within eligible & f011=0 (n=8831): treatment effect +5.00 mo (n_t=1790); within eligible & f011=1 (n=495): +5.24 mo (n_t=113). Indistinguishable; feature_011 is not a further effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 has no main effect on PFS once treatment×eligibility is in the model — its apparent main negative effect was entirely confounded with the unrealized treatment benefit in feature_006=1 patients (because feature_006=1 disqualifies eligibility).

analyses.result_summary: feature_006 main coef = +0.001 (p=0.57) in the model with eligible×feature_027 interaction (vs -0.29 without). Same pattern for feature_023 (-0.003, p=0.14) and feature_022 (+0.001, p=0.85). These three biomarkers act purely as treatment-effect modifiers, NOT prognostic factors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: log(feature_005) is similarly not prognostic on its own once eligibility is modeled — its apparent univariate negative association is driven by treatment-effect heterogeneity.

analyses.result_summary: log(feature_005) coef = +0.001 (p=0.42) in the model with eligible×feature_027 interaction (vs -0.116 without). feature_005 acts as a treatment-effect modifier (the cutoff at 5 separates eligibility), not a prognostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup hypothesis: feature_027 increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months when ALL of the following hold: feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0, and feature_005<5; outside this subgroup, feature_027 has no PFS effect (point estimate ~0, 95% CI excludes any clinically meaningful benefit).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS within eligible vs ineligible
analyses.result_summary: Definitive estimate. Eligible (n=9326): adjusted feature_027 coef = +4.979 (95% CI 4.971-4.987), p~0. Ineligible (n=40674): adjusted feature_027 coef = -0.003 (95% CI -0.008 to +0.002), p=0.24. The full subgroup definition is necessary and sufficient for treatment benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Heterogeneity sweep for any other treatment: no other binary feature behaves as an effect-modifying treatment for pfs_months — feature_027 is the unique treatment with subgroup heterogeneity.

Final summary of the prognostic vs predictive structure: prognostic features are feature_015 (KPS-like), feature_001 (stage 0/1/2), feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, log(feature_021); the predictive (effect-modifying) features for feature_027 are feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, and feature_005 (with a sharp cutoff at 5).

analyses.code: interaction screen for each binary against all covariates; comprehensive subgroup search
analyses.result_summary: Across all candidate binaries (feature_010, 024, 012, 018, 020, 014, 016, 028, 002, 031), no binary except feature_027 shows differential effect across other features (max heterogeneity coefficient < 0.15 in absolute value, vs +5.0 for feature_027 in eligible). feature_027 is the unique treatment with heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Final fully-adjusted OLS with eligibility interaction; R²=0.993
analyses.result_summary: Final model PFS = -6.74 + 0.176*feature_015 - 1.166*feature_001 - 1.369*feature_014 + 0.485*feature_019 - 0.078*feature_025 - 0.097*log(feature_021) + 4.979*(eligible × feature_027) + ε. R²=0.993. The constant + listed prognostic terms explain ~86% of variance; the eligible×treatment interaction adds the final ~13% to reach R²≈0.99.
